2022-05-01.title: Ukrainian officials said they found the bodies of three men in Bucha, with signs they had been ‘tortured.’
2022-05-01.abstract: A police chief said that victims’ bodies were discovered in a forest, and showed signs of mutilation.

2022-05-01.title: Deaths of foreign fighters draw renewed attention to the military volunteers in Ukraine.
2022-05-01.abstract: At least three foreigners have been killed fighting with Ukraine’s International Legion. One analyst said many would-be volunteers underestimated the danger from Russian forces.

2022-05-01.title: Two Interviews, No Regrets: Talking About Putin’s Russia With a Former German Chancellor
2022-05-01.abstract: After Gerhard Schröder spoke to The Times, he could be kicked out of his party and cut off from some tax-financed perks he enjoys as former chancellor.

2022-05-01.title: Inside China’s Zero-Covid Fortress, Xi Admits No Doubts
2022-05-01.abstract: As the lockdown of Shanghai and outbreaks in Beijing and elsewhere batter the economy, China’s leader keeps a distance from the politically charged issue.

2022-05-01.title: Beijing Escalates Restrictions, but Still Stops Short of Lockdown
2022-05-01.abstract: Officials in China’s capital are striving to contain a coronavirus outbreak while avoiding public anger of the kind that has erupted in Shanghai.

2022-05-02.title: U.K. Plan to Send Asylum Seekers to Rwanda Stokes Anger and Dread
2022-05-02.abstract: The government’s proposal has led to recriminations from rights groups, and anxiety among migrants who fear being sent away.

2022-05-02.title: South Africa’s latest surge is a possible preview of the pandemic’s next chapter.
2022-05-02.abstract: The spread of two newly discovered subvariants has doctors watching closely.

2022-05-02.title: Sisters Recount Perilous Escape From Mariupol as Russians Closed In
2022-05-02.abstract: The women say they were forced onto a bus to Russian-controlled territory, then escaped what Ukrainian and U.S. officials have described as “filtration” centers for a system of forced expulsions to Russia.

2022-05-02.title: Beijing reopens a mass isolation center and tries to avoid a citywide lockdown.
2022-05-02.abstract: While the Chinese capital has recorded only a few hundred cases in recent weeks, the move to reopen the facility appears to be aimed at avoiding the fate of Shanghai.

2022-05-02.title: Israel condemns Russian foreign minister’s comments as antisemitic.
2022-05-02.abstract: Israel’s response is among the fiercest criticism it has leveled at the Russian government since the invasion of Ukraine.

2022-05-03.title: Beijing’s Covid Lockdowns: Close to My Home
2022-05-03.abstract: Beijing has been locking down apartment complexes once cases are found. Here is what my district looked like.

2022-05-03.title: Australian Gets 12 Years for Anti-Gay Killing of an American in 1988
2022-05-03.abstract: Scott Johnson, a U.S. graduate student, was pushed off a cliff, in a case that was first ruled a suicide but that his brother refused to let go of.

2022-05-03.title: Cuban Migrants Arrive to U.S. in Record Numbers, on Foot, Not by Boat
2022-05-03.abstract: Political repression and escalating economic difficulties on the island, along with a new visa-free travel policy in Nicaragua, are some of the factors driving the change.

2022-05-03.title: Holy City or Theme Park? Israel Plans Zip Line for Ancient Jerusalem.
2022-05-03.abstract: A Jewish settler group is promoting projects that opponents say will change the delicate historic, religious and political balance in highly symbolic areas around Jerusalem’s Old City.

2022-05-03.title: Readers ask: What are the major sanctions imposed on Russia, and what could be the most crippling?
2022-05-03.abstract: Our reporters answer readers’ questions about Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Many of you asked about economic penalties and how Putin could respond.

2022-05-04.title: North Korea Launches Ballistic Missile Ahead of Yoon’s Inauguration
2022-05-04.abstract: Kim Jong-un has sought to improve the North’s nuclear capabilities and gain diplomatic leverage with Washington and the incoming administration of Yoon Suk-yeol.

2022-05-04.title: The E.U. unveils a plan to ban Russian oil imports.
2022-05-04.abstract: The  proposal will be presented in detail to E.U. ambassadors Wednesday and is expected to be adopted by the end of the week.

2022-05-04.title: In Mexico, One Cartel Is Cleared, but Others Storm In
2022-05-04.abstract: In the state of Michoacán, small drug cartels compete with larger ones for territory, leaving the residents caught in the middle of a brutal turf war.

2022-05-04.title: Japan Says It Needs Nuclear Power. Can Host Towns Ever Trust It Again?
2022-05-04.abstract: The Ukraine war has shown the fragility of Japan’s energy supplies. But the decision to restart plants after the Fukushima disaster is fraught with emotions and political calculation.

2022-05-04.title: A Crumbling Russian ‘Spyville’ Returns to Polish Hands
2022-05-04.abstract: A Warsaw housing complex built in the 1970s and rented by Russia was reputed to be a nest of spies. The city’s government has seized the site and pledged to hand it over to the Ukrainian community.

2022-05-05.title: Stanislav Shushkevich, First Leader of Post-Soviet Belarus, Dies at 87
2022-05-05.abstract: He helped formalize the breakup of the Soviet Union in 1991, led his country until 1994, then became a vocal critic of his successor, Aleksandr G. Lukashenko.

2022-05-05.title: India Finds Russian Oil an Irresistible Deal, No Matter the Diplomatic Pressure
2022-05-05.abstract: A parade of emissaries have urged a harder line on Russia. But India’s political neutrality over the war in Ukraine has expanded into economic opportunism.

2022-05-05.title: U.K. Local Elections Pose Test for Scandal-Prone Boris Johnson
2022-05-05.abstract: The British prime minister is under fire for lockdown-breaking parties. But many voters are skeptical that the opposition can solve issues such as soaring prices.

2022-05-05.title: Elon Musk Left a South Africa That Was Rife With Misinformation and White Privilege
2022-05-05.abstract: The apartheid era created all-white enclaves littered with anti-Black government propaganda and sheltered from the atrocities of apartheid.

2022-05-05.title: Anti-Putin Russians Are Leaving, With a Push From the Kremlin
2022-05-05.abstract: Many Russians who are designated “foreign agents” by the government view it as another form of repression that makes living in the country, or returning to it, untenable.

2022-05-06.title: What were people voting for?
2022-05-06.abstract: Britons voted on Thursday to select thousands of representatives in scores of local municipalities, mostly council members who oversee functions like filling potholes, collecting trash and issuing construction permits.

2022-05-06.title: Divided Unionists have left an opening for Sinn Fein in Northern Ireland.
2022-05-06.abstract: The Irish nationalist party, Sinn Fein, was widely expected to win the most seats in elections for the Northern Ireland legislature on Thursday, an outcome that would represent an extraordinary coming-of-age for a political party that many still associate with years of paramilitary violence.

2022-05-06.title: In a One-Man Race in Hong Kong, China Is Guaranteed to Win
2022-05-06.abstract: John Lee, a Beijing loyalist who oversaw the crackdown on pro-democracy protests, built his career by deepening his contacts within the mainland security services.

2022-05-06.title: One Village at a Time: The Grinding Artillery War in Ukraine
2022-05-06.abstract: The advances by the Ukrainian army in the north have been modest, but they are emblematic of both sides’ strategy: maneuvering artillery to gain territory.

2022-05-06.title: How the King of Rock ’n’ Roll Still Makes Australia Sing
2022-05-06.abstract: Elvis never played a concert “down under,” but that hasn’t stopped tens of thousands of Australians from making him their own at an annual festival.

2022-05-07.title: George Cheeks, the president of CBS, tests positive for coronavirus after attending the White House Correspondents’ Dinner.
2022-05-07.abstract: The network’s leader sat between the president and first lady, all unmasked, at the event.

2022-05-07.title: Lviv reopens art galleries ‘to show we are alive.’
2022-05-07.abstract: As the Russian invasion of Ukraine grinds on, a museum director in Ukraine’s cultural capital is turning to art as a form of resistance.

2022-05-07.title: Russia’s Grave Miscalculation: Ukrainians Would Collaborate
2022-05-07.abstract: Besides their military failures, the Russians entered the war believing they could persuade Ukrainians in the east to join their side. Those efforts have failed spectacularly.

2022-05-07.title: Do All Auto Industry Jobs Rest on Government Support?
2022-05-07.abstract: A former auto industry executive argues that Canada and Ontario should rethink how they subsidize carmakers.

2022-05-07.title: North Korea Tests a Submarine-Launched Missile
2022-05-07.abstract: Such missiles could extend the range of the North’s nuclear arsenal. The move came as the United States warned that the country might soon test a nuclear device.

2022-05-08.title: A Pot of U.N. Money. Risk-Taking Officials. A Sea of Questions.
2022-05-08.abstract: A little-known United Nations agency decided to make an impact by doling out loans and grant money — all to a single family. It did not go well.

2022-05-08.title: Eid Under the Taliban Shows a Changed Afghanistan
2022-05-08.abstract: For many Afghans the holiday this past week served as a reminder of the dissonance between the promise of peace many had imagined and the realities of the end of the war.

2022-05-08.title: Beijing’s Man in Hong Kong Inherits a Tamed City
2022-05-08.abstract: John Lee, who won a rubber-stamp leadership election on Sunday, will implement the next stage of China’s agenda for the former British colony.

2022-05-08.title: Dozens are feared dead after a Russian airstrike hits a school building, officials say.
2022-05-08.abstract: Initial reports suggested that as many as 60 people were trapped inside the building, which officials said was being used as a shelter for civilians.

2022-05-08.title: Israel Captures 2 Palestinians Suspected in Deadly Ax Attack
2022-05-08.abstract: The two men were caught hiding in a forest not far from where the killings were carried out, ending an intensive search. But the country remains on high alert.

2022-05-09.title: A bomb-sniffing dog named Patron received state honors from Zelensky.
2022-05-09.abstract: Patron has found more than 200 explosives, President Zelensky said. The dog has gone viral as Ukraine wages an information war.

2022-05-09.title: It’s the most consequential election in the Philippines in recent history.
2022-05-09.abstract: MANILA — Filipinos headed to the polls on Monday in a hotly contested election that has been described as the most consequential in decades, one that will shape the direction of the country’s fragile democracy and transform alliances with the United States and China.

2022-05-09.title: After 36 years, another Marcos may lead the Philippines.
2022-05-09.abstract: Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has spent decades defending his family’s name against accusations of corruption and downplaying his father’s brutal regime.

2022-05-09.title: Reports of violence and broken voting machines in the Philippines leave voters on edge.
2022-05-09.abstract: Security was tight for this election, a particularly high-stakes contest that some observers have called an existential battle for the soul of the nation.

2022-05-09.title: Leni Robredo, the only woman seeking the presidency, receives a groundswell of support from the young.
2022-05-09.abstract: “Generation Z” and thousands of millennial voters drew inspiration from her effort to take on Ferdinand Marcos Jr.

2022-05-10.title: In Korea, a New President in the South Vows a Harder Line on the North
2022-05-10.abstract: During his inaugural speech, Yoon Suk-yeol said his country would play a greater role in promoting freedom and human rights around the world.

2022-05-10.title: Crowdfunding a War: How Online Appeals Are Bringing Weapons to Ukraine
2022-05-10.abstract: A campaign to crowdfund donations and weapons is producing millions of dollars as well as a bounty of smaller weapons and other military equipment for the Ukrainian military.

2022-05-10.title: For French Military, War in Ukraine Prompts a Shift in Focus
2022-05-10.abstract: While it is in Europe’s interest to weaken Moscow, the chief of staff of France’s military says, Russia may someday need to play a part in a global security plan.

2022-05-10.title: ‘They are really on the last breath’: The wives of soldiers in Mariupol plead for help.
2022-05-10.abstract: Interviews and public comments by the spouses of soldiers holed up in a steel plant in the southern Ukrainian city paint a grim picture of the fighters’ situation.

2022-05-10.title: Readers ask: How are public services operating in Ukraine?
2022-05-10.abstract: Our reporters answer readers’ questions about Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Many of you asked about schools and hospitals.

2022-05-11.title: Leonid Kravchuk, First President of an Independent Ukraine, Dies at 88
2022-05-11.abstract: Days after his election, he signed a multinational agreement that formally declared the dissolution of the Soviet Union.

2022-05-11.title: Al Jazeera Journalist Is Killed in West Bank
2022-05-11.abstract: The network and Palestinian authorities blamed Israeli troops for the killing. Israel said the blame could lie with Palestinian gunmen.

2022-05-11.title: Old Palestinian Wound Resurfaces at Israeli Resort
2022-05-11.abstract: Israeli soldiers had long denied killing prisoners after capturing an Arab seaside town, days after Israel’s creation. A new film provides fresh evidence — reopening a debate about Israel’s foundational story.

2022-05-11.title: Once Best Friends, Bulgaria Takes a Stand Against Russia
2022-05-11.abstract: Bulgaria has imposed economic sanctions on Russia, offered to repair broken military equipment for Ukraine and expelled Russian diplomats.

2022-05-11.title: ‘Perpetual Violence’: India’s Dangerous New Pattern of Communal Tensions
2022-05-11.abstract: As a national campaign by right-wing groups inflames local tensions, Muslim communities are facing the harshest punishments, according to activists, analysts and retired officials.

2022-05-12.title: North Korea reports its first Covid outbreak.
2022-05-12.abstract: The country’s leader, Kim Jong-un, ordered a national lockdown after a subvariant of the Omicron variant of the coronavirus was found in the capital.

2022-05-12.title: Major Donation to U.K. Conservative Party Was Flagged Over Russia Concerns
2022-05-12.abstract: The cash was part of a fund-raising blitz that helped propel Prime Minister Boris Johnson’s party to victory in 2019. Records track $630,225 to a Russian bank account.

2022-05-12.title: Finland’s leaders urge applying for NATO membership ‘without delay.’
2022-05-12.abstract: Public opinion in Finland has shifted significantly in favor of joining the alliance in the 11 weeks since Russia invaded Ukraine.

2022-05-12.title: Life in a Ukrainian Unit: Diving for Cover, Waiting for Western Weapons
2022-05-12.abstract: Analysts say the outcome of fighting now is riding on the accuracy, quantity and the striking power of long-range weapons. Ukraine is pleading for more.

2022-05-12.title: Outside the Walls of a Salvadoran Prison, ‘We’re All Crying Mothers’
2022-05-12.abstract: Dozens of women wait outside a detention facility for news of their loved ones after a crackdown on gang violence resulted in the imprisonment of more than 25,000 people.

2022-05-13.title: North Korea Says 6 Dead as Covid-19 Spreads ‘Explosively’
2022-05-13.abstract: The country’s leader, Kim Jong-un, said 187,800 people were in quarantine, and state TV showed him wearing a mask for the first time.

2022-05-13.title: South Korea Turns to Surveillance as ‘Ghost Surgeries’ Shake Faith in Hospitals
2022-05-13.abstract: After scandals in which doctors let unsupervised assistants operate on patients, the country is becoming one of the first to require cameras in operating rooms.

2022-05-13.title: A Rabbi’s Contentious Quest for Religious Pluralism in Israel
2022-05-13.abstract: There is more than one way to be Jewish, says Gilad Kariv, the first Reform rabbi in Israel’s Parliament. The idea poses a challenge to the country’s Orthodox establishment.

2022-05-13.title: After 200 Years of Neutrality, Sweden Weighs Joining NATO
2022-05-13.abstract: Russia’s invasion of Ukraine created new fears, and the Swedes, dragged along by Finland, are expected to apply, reluctantly, to join the alliance and its collective defense.

2022-05-13.title: Ukraine begins a trial of a Russian soldier accused of a war crime, a first since the conflict began.
2022-05-13.abstract: Sgt. Vadim Shysimarin is accused of shooting a 62-year-old man on a bicycle in a village in the Sumy region, about 200 miles east of Kyiv.

2022-05-14.title: Protests Triggered by Rising Food Prices Spread in Iran
2022-05-14.abstract: “They have no hope, they have no trust in the government and they can’t tolerate the status quo any more,” one Iran expert said of the protesters in at least six provinces.

2022-05-14.title: Jacinda Ardern, whose restrictions buffered New Zealand from the worst of the pandemic, tests positive.
2022-05-14.abstract: The prime minister’s rules kept transmission at bay for two years, and by the time the highly infectious Omicron variant hit, the vast majority of New Zealand’s population had been vaccinated.

2022-05-14.title: Amid war and destruction, Ukraine is favored to win the wildly popular Eurovision Song Contest.
2022-05-14.abstract: The exclusion of Russia from the contest has underlined the country’s estrangement from the west, including in popular culture.

2022-05-14.title: North Korea Says Its Covid Outbreak Is Spreading Fast
2022-05-14.abstract: State media reported 21 new deaths and a huge jump in suspected cases on Saturday, but it was unclear how many had been definitively linked to the coronavirus.

2022-05-14.title: A Ruling Family on the Run as Sri Lanka Plunges Into Economic Ruin
2022-05-14.abstract: Once empowered by triumphant ethnic nationalism after a brutal civil war, the Rajapaksa dynasty has been undone by what its own allies call incompetence and denial.

2022-05-15.title: Somalia Elects New President, but Terrorists Hold True Power
2022-05-15.abstract: The militants of Al Shabab collect taxes, decide court cases and control the streets. Somalis ask, will a new government even matter?

2022-05-15.title: After Lebanon’s Collapse, Can an Election Fix the Country?
2022-05-15.abstract: On Sunday, Lebanese voters got their first chance to pass judgment on lawmakers since the economy fell apart. Few expect things to improve.

2022-05-15.title: How Australia Saved Thousands of Lives While Covid Killed a Million Americans
2022-05-15.abstract: The United States and Australia share similar demographics, but their pandemic death rates point to very different cultures of trust.

2022-05-15.title: A Mini-Russia Gets Squeezed by War
2022-05-15.abstract: The self-declared republic of Transnistria, on the Ukraine border, has been steered by Moscow for decades. During a rare visit, a reporter and photographer get an inside look into how it operates, and hear from Transnistrians their fears of war.

2022-05-15.title: Finland’s president talks of ‘a new era’ as he launches its bid to join NATO.
2022-05-15.abstract: The Nordic nation would end decades of nonalignment if, as widely expected, it is admitted to the alliance.

2022-05-16.title: Pandit Shiv Kumar Sharma, Master of the Santoor, Dies at 84
2022-05-16.abstract: He single-handedly elevated a 100-string instrument little known outside Kashmir into a prominent component of Hindustani classical music.

2022-05-16.title: In Rebirth for NATO, Europe Unites in Face of Putin’s Ambition
2022-05-16.abstract: Sweden’s and Finland’s embrace of NATO after decades of neutrality is a clear statement that Russia’s invasion of Ukraine has ended an era of European illusions about Moscow.

2022-05-16.title: How the Long Recovery From Bush Fires Could Decide Australia’s Election
2022-05-16.abstract: The fires that tore through the country in late 2019 and early 2020 are history, but halting recovery efforts have kept memories vivid and anger fresh.

2022-05-16.title: The West’s Scramble for Gas Could Enrich and Empower Tiny Qatar
2022-05-16.abstract: As the United States and Europe seek to deprive Russia of its oil and gas income, their leaders hope Qatar can help fill the void.

2022-05-16.title: Taking Aim at Left-Leaning Voters, Macron Names a Woman Prime Minister
2022-05-16.abstract: Élisabeth Borne, the minister of labor who previously was in charge of the environment, will be the second woman to hold the post in France.

2022-05-17.title: Michael Bloomberg Plans a $242 Million Investment in Clean Energy
2022-05-17.abstract: The billionaire and former mayor of New York City will fund programs in 10 developing countries.

2022-05-17.title: Most Canadians Don’t Want Charles as King, but Changing Royal Rule Isn’t Easy
2022-05-17.abstract: Prince Charles will make a three day tour of the country, where polls suggest there’s little support for the monarchy — but amending Canada’s Constitution is difficult.

2022-05-17.title: In Bucha, a Symbol of Death and Atrocity, Life Returns
2022-05-17.abstract: About 10,000 residents have come back to this eastern Ukrainian city, which is racing to repair the physical and psychological damage from Russia’s occupation.

2022-05-17.title: Life Returns to Bucha
2022-05-17.abstract: In Bucha, Ukraine, our correspondent saw a city overshadowed by death coming back to life.

2022-05-17.title: Readers ask: How do Ukrainians see the Western response to the war?
2022-05-17.abstract: Our reporters are answering readers’ questions about Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Many of you have asked about sentiments in Ukraine about the West.

2022-05-18.title: Labour asks if Conservatives broke U.K. election laws by accepting a donation linked to a Russian account.
2022-05-18.abstract: Britain’s opposition Labour Party on Tuesday formally demanded answers in Parliament to whether the governing Conservative Party had breached electoral laws when it accepted a major political donation that documents link to a Russian bank account. Opposition leaders also asked for information about the donor’s potential ties to the Kremlin.

2022-05-18.title: ‘I could not stay away’: With Russians pushed back, residents return to Kharkiv.
2022-05-18.abstract: Family members clutched one another in joy at Kharkiv railway station on Tuesday as relatives who had fled the bombardment of the city arrived on the train from the capital, Kyiv.

2022-05-18.title: North Korea Wants to Follow China’s Covid ‘Success.’ Its Plan May Backfire.
2022-05-18.abstract: As infections spread quickly, outside experts are warning that the country’s desire to mimic Beijing’s playbook could worsen a coming disaster.

2022-05-18.title: Around Kharkiv, Ukrainians Emerge to Find Lives in Ruin
2022-05-18.abstract: After sheltering underground since February, residents of the beseiged city have finally been able to to assess the damage the Russians left behind. The discoveries have been grisly.

2022-05-18.title: A Shelter for Displaced Artists in Ukraine
2022-05-18.abstract: On the outskirts of Lviv, displaced artists shunning government shelters have found a home in an abandoned factory. They recently opened up their space.

2022-05-19.title: A Town’s Covid Money Was Sent to One Man in Error. He Gambled It All Away.
2022-05-19.abstract: An official in the rural Japanese town wired the 24-year-old man nearly $360,000 by mistake. He lost it in online casinos. Now, the town is suing and he has been arrested.

2022-05-19.title: An Indian State Banned Alcohol. The Drinking Moved to Nearby Nepal.
2022-05-19.abstract: The ban in Bihar State, championed by local women, was aimed at tackling alcoholism and domestic violence. But a drinking scene has sprung up across the border in Nepal.

2022-05-19.title: The 17th-Century Judge at the Heart of Today’s Women’s Rights Rulings
2022-05-19.abstract: Both in India and in the Roe v. Wade draft ruling roiling the United States, Lord Matthew Hale — an English judge who wrote that women were contractually obligated to husbands — still looms large.

2022-05-19.title: How a Group of Female Independents Aims to Revive Australian Democracy
2022-05-19.abstract: A community-driven movement has recruited around 25 candidates, most of them successful women preaching pragmatic reform. They could shape the balance of power after Saturday’s election.

2022-05-19.title: ‘Wagatha Christie’ Case Offers a Riveting Peek Into Celebrity Culture
2022-05-19.abstract: Libel proceedings in London have laid bare the personal lives of two British celebrities in a legal whodunit for the social media age. And it wasn’t just the tabloids that became obsessed with the feud.

2022-05-20.title: A Russian rock singer was charged after condemning the Ukraine war at a concert.
2022-05-20.abstract: The singer made the comments less than three months after Russia enacted a law that would punish spreading “false information” about its invasion.

2022-05-20.title: As China Doubles Down on Lockdowns, Some Chinese Seek an Exit
2022-05-20.abstract: Inquiries to immigration consultants have surged; social media users trade tips on how to get abroad. But the government aims to “strictly restrict nonessential exit activities.”

2022-05-20.title: In Pictures: Sri Lanka’s Protesters Remain Defiant
2022-05-20.abstract: As the pain of an economic crisis deepens, a nation’s anger and desperation are visible.

2022-05-20.title: Free Ambulances in Mogadishu, Thanks to This Determined Dentist
2022-05-20.abstract: Dismayed that victims of the incessant violence in Somalia’s capital were being rolled to hospitals on wooden carts and in wheelbarrows, a dentist decided to do something about it.

2022-05-20.title: U.N. Human Rights Chief to Make First Trip to China Since 2005
2022-05-20.abstract: Rights groups say Michelle Bachelet’s trip, including a visit to the western Chinese region of Xinjiang, where Beijing has cracked down on the Uyghur minority, will be a test of her office’s credibility.

2022-05-21.title: Prime Minister Scott Morrison is urging voters to opt for what they know.
2022-05-21.abstract: His tenure hasn’t always been smooth, with moments that have tested the Australian public’s faith in his leadership

2022-05-21.title: Anthony Albanese, the challenger, pitches ‘safe change.’
2022-05-21.abstract: The opposition leader has emphasized the good that effective government can do.

2022-05-21.title: Australia decides whether to extend conservatives’ reign.
2022-05-21.abstract: Australia’s federal election is a test of whether the country of 25 million people wants to end or extend nine years of conservative government.

2022-05-21.title: Democracy, with a side of grilled onions.
2022-05-21.abstract: In a beloved tradition, every Election Day sausages are sold near polling stations as fund-raisers for local schools, churches and community groups.

2022-05-21.title: In Australia, skipping the polls comes with a fine.
2022-05-21.abstract: Compulsory voting is seen as forcing candidates toward the political center.

2022-05-22.title: Australia’s ‘Climate Election’ Finally Arrived. Will It Be Enough?
2022-05-22.abstract: Voters rejected the deny-and-delay approach that has made Australia a global laggard on emission cuts. But how far the new government will go remains to be seen.

2022-05-22.title: Documenting Atrocities in the War in Ukraine
2022-05-22.abstract: Russia’s invasion of Ukraine has generated enormous demand in both Ukraine and friendly Western governments for an accounting of Russian military actions that could be prosecuted as war crimes — actions so  egregious they violate the commonly accepted laws concerning armed combat.

2022-05-22.title: 1897: Cambridge University Votes Against Degrees for Women
2022-05-22.abstract: A resolution to grant degrees to women was rejected by a large majority and men celebrated the result with fireworks.

2022-05-22.title: ‘Such Bad Guys Will Come’: How One Russian Brigade Terrorized Bucha
2022-05-22.abstract: A particularly fearsome unit of Moscow’s invading army arrived in the Kyiv suburb in mid-March. The soldiers’ reputation preceded them.

2022-05-22.title: Millions Displaced and Dozens Dead in Flooding in India and Bangladesh
2022-05-22.abstract: Heavy rains have washed away towns, villages and infrastructure, as extreme weather events become more common in South Asia.

2022-05-23.title: Biden Pledges to Defend Taiwan if It Faces a Chinese Attack
2022-05-23.abstract: Moving away from a policy of “strategic ambiguity,” the president said that to protect the island democracy, he would go beyond what the U.S. has done for Ukraine.

2022-05-23.title: Biden to Begin New Asia-Pacific Economic Bloc With a Dozen Allies
2022-05-23.abstract: The goal is a resurgence in U.S. influence in the region, especially vis-à-vis China, after President Donald J. Trump’s withdrawal from the Trans-Pacific Partnership.

2022-05-23.title: Seven Dead and More Than 100 Rescued in Philippine Ferry Fire
2022-05-23.abstract: The vessel was carrying 124 passengers and 10 crew members when it caught fire soon after setting off at dawn from Polillo Island.

2022-05-23.title: The Refinery Standing Between Germany and an Oil Embargo
2022-05-23.abstract: A Russian-owned refinery, linked to a Soviet-era pipeline, is a reminder of Germany’s past and a stumbling block to efforts to cut off Russian oil.

2022-05-23.title: In Haiti, ‘imagining what it could have been.’
2022-05-23.abstract: Some Haitians say the world needs to understand the history that brought them to their present-day straits.

2022-05-24.title: Russia’s Shrinking War
2022-05-24.abstract: The goals of Russia’s invasion keep getting smaller. But its depleted military is still failing to make major advances, and time is on Ukraine’s side.

2022-05-24.title: Want to Host a Ukrainian Refugee? The U.K. Isn’t Making It Easy
2022-05-24.abstract: A plan that allows Britons to sponsor people fleeing the Russian invasion has been plagued by visa delays and safeguarding concerns.

2022-05-24.title: Biden’s Words on Taiwan Leave Allies in an Awkward Spot
2022-05-24.abstract: Ahead of a summit with Australia, India and Japan, an off-the-cuff vow to defend the island militarily against China complicates diplomacy, particularly for Australia.

2022-05-24.title: War Raises Famine Fears as Russia Chokes Off Ukraine’s Farms and Exports
2022-05-24.abstract: World leaders say there is no easy way to bypass a Russian blockade without a military clash, and accuse Vladimir V. Putin of using global hunger as a weapon.

2022-05-24.title: In a Remote Thai Village, Digging for Clues to a Lost Pilot
2022-05-24.abstract: In 1944, a U.S. military plane crashed in a spot the locals named “Deadman’s Torso Flat.” A team has found remains that it hopes are those of the airman.

2022-05-25.title: How (and Why) We Calculated the Value of Haiti’s Payments to France
2022-05-25.abstract: We wanted to know how much Haiti had paid over generations. So we set out to compile the data.

2022-05-25.title: A Heat Wave’s Lamented Victim: The Mango, India’s King of Fruits
2022-05-25.abstract: Blistering spring temperatures have devastated crops of the country’s most beloved fruit. “The soul of a farmer shudders at seeing these fruitless trees,” one grower said.

2022-05-25.title: China is organizing military drills near Taiwan in a warning to the U.S.
2022-05-25.abstract: After President Biden suggested the United States would get involved militarily to defend Taiwan if China attacked it, China described organizing combat drills in the seas and airspace around Taiwan.

2022-05-25.title: The Downing Street lockdown parties ‘represent a serious failure,’ a report says.
2022-05-25.abstract: LONDON — Prime Minister Boris Johnson presided over a disorderly workplace in which there were widespread violations of coronavirus restrictions, according to a long-awaited government investigation released on Wednesday that is a moment of reckoning for the scandal-scarred British leader.

2022-05-25.title: Read the report on lockdown parties in Downing Street.
2022-05-25.abstract: A senior British civil servant, Sue Gray, compiled a report on parties held in Downing Street in violation of Covid lockdown rules. The scandal has put pressure on Prime Minister Boris Johnson.

2022-05-26.title: 11 Newborns Killed in Hospital Fire, Senegal’s Leader Says
2022-05-26.abstract: President Macky Sall said the blaze broke out in a neonatal unit, but gave no further details.

2022-05-26.title: Film on Expulsion of Kashmir’s Hindus Is Polarizing and Popular in India
2022-05-26.abstract: Called propaganda by critics and essential viewing by fans, “The Kashmir Files,” an unexpected blockbuster, has drawn the support of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata Party.

2022-05-26.title: Will Colombia Elect Its First Leftist Leader?
2022-05-26.abstract: A large and loud youth electorate hungry to transform one of Latin America’s most unequal societies could propel Gustavo Petro, a former rebel, to the presidency.

2022-05-26.title: In China, concerns grow over the economic impact of ‘zero Covid.’
2022-05-26.abstract: A senior leader announced efforts to bolster growth and urged local leaders do more to keep the economy running.

2022-05-26.title: Johnson Takes Aim at Next Big Political Threat: Soaring Prices in U.K.
2022-05-26.abstract: Prime Minister Boris Johnson, under pressure over a scathing report on lockdown parties in Downing Street and with his party trailing in opinion polls, unveiled an aid package Thursday.

2022-05-27.title: Why You’re Hearing More About the Pacific Islands
2022-05-27.abstract: The United States and Australia are in a contest for influence with China across the region. And it’s intensifying.

2022-05-27.title: 11 Families Had Just Welcomed Newborns. A Hospital Fire Left Them Devastated.
2022-05-27.abstract: Joy over the births turned to shock and grief after a blaze swept through a neonatal ward at a hospital in Senegal. “There’s nothing we can do but suffer,” said a man who lost his grandson.

2022-05-27.title: As U.N. Rights Chief Visits China, Some Fear She’ll Become Part of the Spin
2022-05-27.abstract: Michelle Bachelet’s tour includes Xinjiang, where China has been accused of genocide. The terms of her visit are unclear, and critics say Beijing is using her for propaganda.

2022-05-27.title: Deep in Vatican Archives, Scholar Discovers ‘Flabbergasting’ Secrets
2022-05-27.abstract: David Kertzer has spent decades excavating the Vatican’s hidden history, with his work winning a Pulitzer and capturing Hollywood’s attention. A new book examines Pope Pius XII’s role in the Holocaust.

2022-05-27.title: In hopes of returning home, displaced Ukrainians learn how to detect mines.
2022-05-27.abstract: Courses in western Ukraine, meant for volunteer defenders, are now open to Ukrainian civilians whose villages have been blighted by war.

2022-05-28.title: Drones. Crutches. Potatoes. Russians Crowdfund Their Army.
2022-05-28.abstract: A grass-roots movement to get basic supplies to soldiers fighting in Ukraine reflects the growing recognition among Russians that their military was unprepared for major conflict.

2022-05-28.title: The Fall of the ‘Sun King’ of French TV, and the Myth of Seduction
2022-05-28.abstract: Patrick Poivre d’Arvor, known as a great seducer, has been accused by more than 20 women of rape, sexual assault and harassment in France’s belated #MeToo reckoning.

2022-05-28.title: Ukrainian Orthodox Church Breaks With Moscow Over War
2022-05-28.abstract: The Council of the Ukrainian Orthodox Church said that it disagreed with the Moscow Patriarch’s position on the war.

2022-05-28.title: What Canada Doesn’t Know About Its Guns
2022-05-28.abstract: New record-keeping requirements for nonrestricted firearms began this month, but the origins of Canada’s so-called crime guns are largely unknown.

2022-05-28.title: An Irish National Treasure Gets Set for a Long-Needed Restoration
2022-05-28.abstract: The majestic Old Library at Trinity College Dublin, where some of Ireland’s most ancient and valuable books are stored, is a popular tourist attraction.

2022-05-29.title: Here are the latest developments in the war in Ukraine.
2022-05-29.abstract: Ukraine, newly armed with Western weapons, is making a desperate attempt to hold its ground as Russian forces are close to capturing Sievierodonetsk, the last Ukrainian-controlled city in the Luhansk region, a province now at the heart of the conflict.

2022-05-29.title: Plane Crashes in Nepal With 22 Aboard, Government Says
2022-05-29.abstract: The search for the plane and its passengers and crew was being hampered by bad weather.

2022-05-29.title: A Cafe Devoted to Remembering Duterte’s Drug War Victims
2022-05-29.abstract: Served along with the lattes are personal stories from the staff, who are relatives of those who died in some of the thousands of extrajudicial killings in the Philippines.

2022-05-29.title: Green Energy Complicates the Taliban’s New Battle Against Opium
2022-05-29.abstract: The multibillion-dollar trade has survived previous bans. Now, the Taliban are going after solar-powered water pumps to try to dry up poppy crops in the middle of a national economic crisis.

2022-05-29.title: As Boris Johnson Stumbles, Labour Struggles to Offer a Clear Message
2022-05-29.abstract: Out of power for 12 years, Britain’s Labour Party has made some gains, but its message hasn’t won back the rust belt regions that abandoned it in the last election.

2022-05-30.title: In Colombia, a Leftist and a Right-Wing Populist Head for June Runoff
2022-05-30.abstract: The results in the first round of voting delivered a stunning blow to Colombia’s dominant conservative political class.

2022-05-30.title: Here are the latest developments in the war in Ukraine.
2022-05-30.abstract: A day after fighting in Ukraine expanded on another front, with the military announcing a counteroffensive around the southern port city of Kherson, European leaders will be gathering in Brussels for a two-day summit focused on the war in Ukraine.

2022-05-30.title: All 22 Bodies Are Recovered in Nepal Plane Crash
2022-05-30.abstract: The plane went down in bad weather on Sunday while carrying passengers to Jomsom, a tourist destination popular with trekkers.

2022-05-30.title: Canada’s Military, Where Sexual Misconduct Went to the Top, Looks for a New Path
2022-05-30.abstract: A report by a former Supreme Court justice released Monday offers recommendations to turn around pervasive sexual assault and harassment.

2022-05-30.title: A Russian Plane Crashed Into a House. Death Was Parceled Out Randomly.
2022-05-30.abstract: Some fortuitous timing in doing chores saved a family that lived in the Ukrainian city of Chernihiv. A few blocks away, things turned out very differently.

2022-05-31.title: E.U. reaches agreement on a Russian oil import ban.
2022-05-31.abstract: BRUSSELS — European Union leaders have reached a landmark political agreement to ban the vast majority of Russian oil imports by the end of the year, a measure that was considered impossible in the early stages of the Russian invasion of Ukraine because of the bloc’s high dependency on the fuel.

2022-05-31.title: Here are the latest developments in the war in Ukraine.
2022-05-31.abstract: The European Union reached an agreement late Monday to ban most imports of Russian oil, a move that further severs ties between Europe and Russia and marks the harshest economic retribution Moscow has faced for its war in Ukraine.

2022-05-31.title: Crude, Ugly and Pro-American? China Investigates Images in Math Textbooks.
2022-05-31.abstract: The discovery of what some viewed as disturbing illustrations in books for elementary school students set off a national furor.

2022-05-31.title: Even as Russia Bears Down in the East, Some Ukrainians Stay Behind
2022-05-31.abstract: The Russian offensive grinds on, but not everyone is leaving the eastern Donbas region.

2022-05-31.title: Why China Is Miles Ahead in a Pacific Race for Influence
2022-05-31.abstract: To many observers, the South Pacific today reveals what American decline looks like. Even as Washington tries to step up its game, it is still far behind, mistaking speeches for impact and interest for influence.
